alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the method delivers performance comparable to state-of-the-art multi-view clustering methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed method is comparable to state-of-the-art multi-view clustering methods.
alignments.rationale: Direct restatement of the same performance claim with no meaningful change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first effort to design an interpretable clustering framework specifically for multi-view data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first effort to design an interpretable clustering framework specifically for multi-view data.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extended TREC Interactive Knowledge Assistance Track (iKAT) collection enables researchers to test and evaluate their Conversational Search Agents (CSA).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extended TREC Interactive Knowledge Assistance Track (iKAT) collection enables researchers to test and evaluate their Conversational Search Agent (CSA).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The collection contains a set of 36 personalized dialogues over 20 different topics, each coupled with a Personal Text Knowledge Base (PTKB).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The collection provides assessments on relevance and additional assessments on generated responses over four key dimensions: relevance, completeness, groundedness, and naturalness.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This research introduces a novel ensemble learning approach for code similarity assessment that combines multiple unsupervised similarity measures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: Identical technical contribution is present in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method facilitates an approximation of the likelihood when dealing with a small number of observations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates computational efficiency compared to using the original VAE while maintaining adequate accuracy for practical applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach demonstrates computational efficiency compared to using the original VAE while maintaining adequate accuracy for practical applications.
alignments.rationale: Performance/efficiency claim retained verbatim in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is particularly suitable for high-dimensional correlated simultaneous observations applicable to various dynamic analysis models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is particularly suitable for high-dimensional correlated simultaneous observations applicable to various dynamic analysis models.
alignments.rationale: Applicability claim preserved without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT4Rec, a Graph Prompt Tuning method for streaming Recommendation, effectively addresses the challenges of adapting to evolving user preferences and new items.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT4Rec, a Graph Prompt Tuning method for streaming Recommendation, effectively addresses the challenges of adapting to evolving user preferences and new items.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method utilizes lightweight graph prompts to guide the model across varying interaction patterns within the user-item graph.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method utilizes lightweight graph prompts to efficiently guide the model across varying interaction patterns within the user-item graph.
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the adverb "efficiently," strengthening the efficiency implication of the claim (a clarification that amplifies performance expectations).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on four diverse real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of GPT4Rec.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on four diverse real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of GPT4Rec.
alignments.rationale: Experimental claim is retained verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GPT4Rec employs node-level, structure-level, and view-level prompts to adapt to changes in graph attributes and relationships.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GPT4Rec employs node-level, structure-level, and view-level prompts to adapt to changes in graph attributes and relationships.
alignments.rationale: Contribution-level description is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed performance modeling methodology significantly reduces the number of training samples while maintaining good accuracy.
alignments.rationale: None of the publication claims correspond to performance modeling or training-sample reduction; the published items describe conference proceedings rather than the preprint’s technical contributions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology achieves a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of as low as 0.02% for single-layer estimations and 0.68% for whole DNN estimations with less than 10000 training samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not report any model performance metrics; the preprint numerical results are absent from the published proceedings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the superiority of our method for single-layer estimations compared to models trained with randomly sampled datasets of the same size.
alignments.rationale: There is no corresponding comparative-model claim in the publication, which focuses on conference metadata rather than the preprint’s experimental comparisons.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proceedings include 24 full papers, 10 full papers in 2 special sessions, and 4 poster sessions.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level descriptive claim does not appear in the preprint and is newly present in the published proceedings.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The papers were carefully reviewed and selected from a total of 57 submissions.
alignments.rationale: This administrative/result claim about submissions is not present in the preprint and is newly introduced in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The conference covers topics related to embedded computer systems, including applications, architectures, and design automation methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists conference topics that are not part of the preprint’s technical claims, so this is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Digital Twin serves as a 'digital playground' to manage the complexity of production systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Digital Twin serves as a 'digital playground' to manage the complexity of production systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same phrasing and meaning as the preprint claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Behavior models within the Digital Twin can provide benefits throughout the entire machine life cycle.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Behavior models within the Digital Twin can provide benefits throughout the entire machine life cycle.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim in the publication, preserving meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The creation and adaptation of behavior models is time-consuming, necessitating improved cost-benefit approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The creation and adaptation of behavior models is time-consuming, necessitating improved cost-benefit approaches.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a lack of specific use cases demonstrating the application and benefits of behavior models in industry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a lack of specific use cases demonstrating the application and benefits of behavior models in industry.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents various realized use cases that illustrate the advantages and applications of behavior models across different life cycle phases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents various realized use cases that illustrate the advantages and applications of behavior models across different life cycle phases.
alignments.rationale: The reported results and use-case presentations appear unchanged between preprint and published version.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The BIMOS model outperforms the BMOS model in terms of team scoring prediction accuracy.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The BMOS model adapts principles from the OBSO model, originally designed for soccer, to basketball.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes BMOS’s lineage/adaptation rather than the broader statement that both BMOS and BIMOS are designed for off-ball scoring prediction, which narrows and clarifies the original contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BIMOS model incorporates the likelihood of interception during ball movements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The BIMOS model incorporates the likelihood of interception during ball movements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of the models was conducted using player tracking data from 630 NBA games in the 2015-2016 regular season.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the described dataset/evaluation detail; instead it emphasizes conceptual contributions and insights, so the specific evaluation claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models provide valuable insights for tactical analysis and player evaluation in basketball.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a concluding claim about the models’ utility for tactical analysis that is not present in the preprint and thus is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable property-based systems have been shown to provide explanations in plain terms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explainable property-based systems provide explanations in plain terms but do not perform as well as leading unexplainable machine learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The explanatory aspect is retained verbatim in the publication’s combined statement; the content of the preprint claim is preserved within the broader published sentence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Explainable property-based systems have not performed as well as leading unexplainable machine learning methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Explainable property-based systems provide explanations in plain terms but do not perform as well as leading unexplainable machine learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication consolidates this performance limitation with the explanatory claim, but the negative performance assertion remains unchanged in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research contributes two methods yielding performance gains in explainability metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research contributes two methods that yield performance gains in explainability metrics.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution claim about two methods and their performance gains in explainability metrics without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first method introduces a combination of explainable and unexplainable flows, proposing a metric to characterize explainability of a decision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The first method introduces a combination of explainable and unexplainable flows, proposing a metric to characterize explainability of a decision.
alignments.rationale: The first-method description and its proposed metric are present in the publication with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second method poses a new metric as the leading performer for estimating the effectiveness of neural networks in the system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The second method poses a new metric as the leading performer for estimating the effectiveness of neural networks in the system.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint’s second-method claim about the new metric’s leading performance.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Results from the new methods and examples from handwritten datasets are presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit statement about empirical results and handwritten-dataset examples that is not present in the preprint’s claim list, so this is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diffusion models can generate 3D molecular structures with specific properties crucial to drug discovery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diffusion models can generate 3D molecular structures with specific properties crucial to drug discovery.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the preprint’s main result claim about diffusion models and 3D molecular generation without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diffusion models were successful at learning complex probability distributions of 3D molecular geometries and their corresponding chemical and physical properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Diffusion models were successful at learning the complex probability distributions of 3D molecular geometries and their corresponding chemical and physical properties.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint’s technical observation about diffusion models learning complex distributions with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This review summarizes the state-of-the-art diffusion models for 3D molecular generation and their role in advancing drug discovery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This review summarizes the state-of-the-art diffusion models for 3D molecular generation and their role in advancing drug discovery.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review discusses challenges associated with generating stable 3D molecular structures using diffusion models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review covers challenges associated with generating stable 3D molecular structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the discussion of challenges for generating stable 3D structures, matching the preprint’s limitation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning-based framework introduced in this paper predicts the mean opinion score with 95.8% accuracy using a dataset of over 20,000 records.

alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the stated 95.8% absolute accuracy on the same dataset; instead it reframes performance relative to ITU P.1203 and emphasizes a reduced-parameter approach, changing the metric and narrowing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework addresses the limitations of existing QoE models by integrating real-time data collection and machine learning predictions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach enhances QoE evaluation accuracy and allows dynamic network resource management, optimizing performance and cost-efficiency.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The open-source nature of the framework encourages adaptation and extension for various multimedia services.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed open-source framework achieves high accuracy in predicting Mean Opinion Scores (MOS) using only key network parameters.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication state the framework is open-source and intended for adaptation; the publication additionally highlights achieved accuracy but preserves the open-source claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings significantly affect the telecommunications industry in managing and optimizing multimedia services.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not make the broader claim about significant industry-wide effects; this broader impact statement does not appear in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework, I Know How (IKH), provides a common formalization for Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A retrospective analysis of anomalies can significantly enhance our comprehension of the underlying causes of observed undesired behavior.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The semi-supervised learning approach generates pseudo-labels for unlabelled point clouds, significantly increasing the amount of training data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of good weather data in the training pipeline allows for high performance in both good and adverse weather conditions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates competitive performance against fully supervised methods while using only a fraction of labeled data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based methodologies deploy neural networks to embed view-invariant attributes. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Innovative solutions have been formulated to tackle the challenges in cross-view geo-localization. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper provides a thorough survey of cutting-edge methodologies, techniques, and associated challenges integral to cross-view image geo-localization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology effectively detects harmful memes in a multicultural and multilingual context. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system achieves an AUROC of 0.7749 and an accuracy of 0.7087, outperforming other teams in the Online Safety Prize Challenge. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report these numeric performance claims; the specific results appear absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach surpasses previous benchmarks, with FLAVA and VisualBERT achieving AUROCs of 0.5695 and 0.5561, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Harmful memes can perpetuate stereotypes, foster discrimination, and exacerbate social divisions across various social dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: AI Singapore has launched the Online Safety Prize Challenge to stimulate research on technologies for detecting harmful memes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new algorithm HyLinUCB incurs only O(√T) regret for T rounds under the diversity assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HyLinUCB incurs only O~(T)$$	ilden{O}(	ext{sqrt}(T))$$ regret under the same diversity assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same regret guarantee for HyLinUCB (notation differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HyLinUCB has significantly lower regret than LinUCB, DisLinUCB, and other state-of-the-art baselines in various situations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HyLinUCB has significantly lower regret than LinUCB, DisLinUCB, and other state of the art baselines in most situations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same comparative performance claim; minor wording change ('various' vs 'most') does not alter the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DisLinUCB incurs the lowest regret when the number of arm specific parameters is much larger than the number of shared parameters. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DisLinUCB incurs the lowest regret when the number of arm specific parameters is much larger than the number of shared parameters. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conditional result for DisLinUCB.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The regret of HyLinUCB grows much slower with the number of arms compared to baselines, making it suitable for very large action spaces. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The regret of HyLinUCB grows much slower with the number of arms K compared to baselines, making it suitable for very large action spaces. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: New regret analyses for both LinUCB and DisLinUCB significantly improve upon the known regret guarantees of these algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about new analyses for LinUCB and DisLinUCB is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In-depth model analysis and clinical case studies validate the improved utilities and insightful interpretations delivered by TACCO.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In-depth model analysis and clinical case studies validate the improved utilities and insightful interpretations delivered by TACCO.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about model analysis and clinical case studies with similar certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Effective prompt designs are essential for improving the accuracy and consistency of LLM-based evaluations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)-based model effectively encodes and reconstructs the observed ships' motion patterns.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Joint angle differences averaged 2.3±2.8° when using the same marker set, compared to 4.5±2.9° otherwise.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our study introduces a novel method for using an RGBD camera as a low-cost motion capture solution.

alignments.rationale: Both statements present the same contribution (a novel, low-cost/consumer-grade RGBD-based motion-capture method) with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study assesses the benefits and limitations of microphone-based sensors as compared to other forms of pedestrian sensing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of a novel sparse ordinal loss substantially improves detail generation in shift-and-scale-invariant monocular depth estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a novel sparse ordinal loss substantially improves detail generation in SSI monocular depth estimation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach demonstrates practical utility in computational photography applications by generating highly detailed scale-invariant depth maps and achieving generalization in diverse scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach demonstrates practical utility in computational photography applications by generating highly detailed SI depth maps and achieving generalization in diverse scenarios.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Generative Gaussian Heads (GGHead) adopts the recent 3D Gaussian Splatting representation within a 3D GAN framework.

alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried over verbatim, indicating no change in meaning or strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NORAN provides a hyper view towards KG modeling, capturing correlations between relations as entity-independent logical evidence. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: NORAN provides a hyper view towards KG modeling, capturing correlations between relations as entity-independent logical evidence. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The conceptual claim about NORAN's modeling perspective is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiment results show that NORAN substantially outperforms state-of-the-art KGC methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that NORAN substantially outperforms state-of-the-art KGC methods on five benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds specificity ("on five benchmarks"), making the experimental claim more concrete and thus narrowing its scope while preserving its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Wearable devices running Machine Learning algorithms can provide objective metrics for tracking chronic cough symptoms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Wearable devices running Machine Learning algorithms can provide objective metrics for tracking chronic cough symptoms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution/claim is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional cough counting algorithms focus on distinguishing cough from non-cough samples, which does not yield clinically relevant outcomes. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a mismatch between state-of-the-art metrics for cough counting algorithms and clinically relevant outcomes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the limitation more generally (metrics/outcomes) rather than the specific focus on sample discrimination; wording is broader and stated with slightly lower confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Event-based evaluation metrics aligned with clinical guidelines are proposed as a better approach for cough counting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Event-based evaluation metrics aligned with clinical guidelines are necessary for assessing algorithm performance in cough counting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the proposal as a necessity for assessment; the intent is the same though slightly stronger in wording, so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional sample-based accuracy measurements can be biased by dataset class imbalance and sample window length. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional sample-based accuracy measurements for cough counting algorithms are biased by dataset class imbalance and sample window length. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The empirical result is restated essentially identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An open-source event-based evaluation framework is presented to test algorithm performance in identifying cough events. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An open-source event-based evaluation framework is presented to test algorithm performance in identifying cough events. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution of an open-source framework is preserved without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UroBot-4o achieved an average Rate of Correct Answers (RoCA) of 88.4%, surpassing GPT-4o by 10.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UroBot-4o achieved the highest mean rate of correct answers (RoCA) at 88.4%, outperforming GPT-4o by 10.8%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UroBot exhibited the highest run agreement as indicated by Fleiss' Kappa (k = 0.979). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UroBot demonstrated the highest level of agreement between runs as measured by Fleiss' kappa (κ = 0.979). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Agreement metric and value are reported identically, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average performance of urologists on board questions is reported to be 68.7%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average performance of urologists on urological board questions is reported to be 68.7%. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported average performance value is retained exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UroBot's clinician-verifiable nature and superior accuracy compared to both existing models and urologists highlight its potential for clinical integration. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: UroBot is a clinician-verifiable and accurate software pipeline that outperforms published models and urologists in answering urology board questions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the conclusion more explicit and stronger (higher certainty and explicit claim of outperforming models and urologists), so the claim is clarified and confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compound AI systems (CASs) are subject to data discovery challenges due to silos of multimodal data sources. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compound AI systems (CASs) are subject to data discovery challenges similar to those faced by data analysts, due to silos of multimodal data sources. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a comparative phrase (similar to data analysts) that clarifies the context but does not change the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing data discovery benchmarks do not model the multimodality and multiplicity of data sources encountered in CASs. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing data discovery benchmarks do not adequately model the multimodality and multiplicity of data sources in CASs. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is restated with equivalent meaning and emphasis in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed CMDBench benchmark models the complexity of enterprise data platforms to evaluate data discovery performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed CMDBench benchmark models the complexity of enterprise data platforms to evaluate data discovery performance in CASs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is preserved; publication adds explicit mention of CASs but does not change substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments reveal a 46% drop in task accuracy on average due to the impact of data retriever design on downstream task performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments reveal a 46% drop in task accuracy on average due to the impact of data retriever design on downstream task performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result and magnitude are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need to develop optimization strategies for identifying appropriate LLM agents and retrievers for efficient execution of CASs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate a need for optimization strategies to identify appropriate LLM agents and retrievers for efficient execution of CASs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is retained with nearly identical meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed EGAB updates demonstrate considerable flexibility by controlling iteration behavior and performance through three hyperparameters: α, β, and the learning rate η. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The EGAB framework provides enhanced flexibility for processing data with varying distributions due to the tunable hyperparameters of the AB divergence. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes flexibility in terms of AB-divergence tunable hyperparameters rather than explicitly listing three parameters; the idea is similar but wording and stated certainty differ.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalized EGAB algorithms enforce a unit l1 norm constraint for nonnegative weight vectors using two distinct approaches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces a novel family of generalized Exponentiated Gradient updates derived from the alpha-beta divergence regularization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes derivation from alpha-beta divergence rather than the specific l1 norm enforcement and two approaches described in the preprint, indicating a change in technical framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Simulation results confirm that the adaptability of the generalized gradient updates can effectively enhance performance for some portfolios, particularly in scenarios involving transaction costs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed EGAB algorithms outperform existing OLPS algorithms in terms of cumulative return, average excess return, Sharpe ratio, and Calmar ratio. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports stronger, multi-metric outperformance against existing algorithms, expanding and strengthening the preprint's more cautious simulator-based claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The EGAB algorithms enable interpolation between various algorithms in online portfolio selection. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This interpolation/algorithmic connection is present in the publication but was not stated in the preprint, so it is a newly added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PRICE is a PRetrained multI-table CardEst model that addresses deployment challenges of existing ML-based CardEst methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PRICE, a PRetrained multI-table CardEst model, addresses deployment challenges of existing ML-based CardEst methods by using low-level transferable features.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but adds a specific mechanism ('low-level transferable features'), which clarifies how PRICE addresses deployment challenges without changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PRICE achieves high estimation accuracy on unseen databases while maintaining a low preparation cost comparable to one-dimensional histogram-based methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PRICE achieves high estimation accuracy on unseen databases while maintaining a preparation cost comparable to basic one-dimensional histogram-based CardEst methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the result but slightly qualifies the comparison ('basic' one-dimensional histograms), a minor clarification that does not alter scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluations show that PRICE consistently outperforms existing methods, achieving the highest estimation accuracy and generating faster execution plans with lower overhead.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluations show that PRICE consistently outperforms existing methods, achieving the highest estimation accuracy and generating faster execution plans with lower overhead.
alignments.rationale: The result is reproduced verbatim in the publication with no substantive change in wording, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: After finetuning with a small volume of database-specific queries, PRICE can find plans very close to the optimal ones.
alignments.pub_claim_text: After finetuning with a small volume of database-specific queries, PRICE can find plans that are very close to optimal ones.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about finetuning yielding near-optimal plans with only minor wording differences, so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PRICE is generally applicable to different settings such as data updates, data scaling, and query workload shifts.
alignments.rationale: This applicability/conclusion about various settings does not appear in the preprint claims and is therefore an added publication claim expanding the paper's stated applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hybrid ray-tracing and wave-propagation model enables accurate simulation of optical aberrations and diffractive phase modulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid ray-tracing and wave-propagation model enables accurate simulation of optical aberrations and diffractive phase modulation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core result directly with no substantive change in content or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model is fully differentiable, allowing for gradient back-propagation in the optimization of refractive-diffractive lenses and image reconstruction networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fully differentiable nature of the proposed model enables gradient back-propagation for end-to-end co-design of lens optimization and image reconstruction.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the differentiability claim as enabling 'end-to-end co-design', clarifying application while lowering reported certainty (medium vs high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the proposed model is validated by comparing simulated point spread functions with theoretical results and showing improved accuracy over commercial software like Zemax.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accuracy of the proposed model is validated by comparing simulated point spread functions with theoretical results and showing improved accuracy over commercial software like Zemax.
alignments.rationale: Validation via PSF comparison and improved accuracy versus Zemax is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-world experiments demonstrate significant improvements in aberration correction and extended depth-of-field imaging using the proposed model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model demonstrates significant improvements in aberration correction and extended depth-of-field imaging through real-world experiments.
alignments.rationale: The real-world experimental results are expressed equivalently in the publication, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model is expected to motivate further investigation into applications in computational imaging, computational photography, and advanced optical design.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's explicit expectation about motivating further investigation does not appear in the publication claims, so this forward-looking/contributory statement was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper aims to merge the local training technique with the adaptive approach to develop efficient distributed learning methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper aims to merge the local training technique with the adaptive approach to develop efficient distributed learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The stated aim to combine local training and adaptive approaches is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classical Local SGD method is enhanced with a scaling feature to improve distributed learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classical Local SGD method is enhanced with a scaling feature to improve distributed optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces 'distributed learning' with 'distributed optimization', a modest reframing/clarification of scope without changing the claim's substance or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scaling feature is described generically, allowing for the analysis of various approaches, including Adam, RMSProp, and OASIS, in a unified manner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scaling feature is described generically, allowing for the analysis of various approaches, including Adam, RMSProp, and OASIS, in a unified manner.
alignments.rationale: The generic description enabling unified analysis of multiple adaptive methods is retained without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed methods is validated in practice by training a neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed methods is validated in practice by training a neural network.
alignments.rationale: The empirical validation by neural network training appears identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Visual geolocalization is emerging as a viable alternative to Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) for aerial localization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Visual geolocalization is emerging as a viable alternative to Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS) for aerial localization.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about visual geolocalization as an emerging alternative is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adapting Visual Place Recognition (VPR) to aerial imagery presents significant challenges, including weather variations and repetitive patterns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adapting Visual Place Recognition (VPR) to aerial imagery presents significant challenges, including weather variations and repetitive patterns.
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding adapting VPR to aerial imagery is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a methodology tailored for evaluating VPR techniques specifically in the domain of aerial imagery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces a methodology tailored for evaluating VPR techniques specifically in the domain of aerial imagery.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of a tailored evaluation methodology is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selecting appropriate zoom and overlap levels when constructing map tiles is important for achieving maximum efficiency of VPR algorithms in aerial imagery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selecting appropriate zoom and overlap levels when constructing map tiles is important for achieving maximum efficiency of VPR algorithms in aerial imagery.
alignments.rationale: The practical result about tile zoom and overlap is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method facilitates fast and scalable kernel matrix embedding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method facilitates fast and scalable kernel matrix embedding.
alignments.rationale: The contribution concerning fast, scalable kernel matrix embedding is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method seamlessly integrates multiple kernels to enhance the learning process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method seamlessly integrates multiple kernels to enhance the learning process.
alignments.rationale: The multi-kernel integration claim is directly preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method demonstrates superior running time compared to standard approaches such as support vector machines and two-layer neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirically, our method demonstrates superior running time compared to standard approaches such as support vector machines and two-layer neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the word 'Empirically,' clarifying that the superior running time is an empirical observation; this is a minor clarification without scope change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieves comparable classification accuracy across various simulated and real datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieves comparable classification accuracy across various simulated and real datasets.
alignments.rationale: The accuracy claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of large language models (LLMs) in summarizing radiology report impressions remains unclear.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current LLMs can produce radiology impressions with high completeness and correctness but fall short in conciseness and verisimilitude.
alignments.rationale: The preprint hedges that effectiveness is unclear, while the publication makes a firmer, positive/negative assessment of specific strengths/weaknesses, so the claim was revised into a more definite conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results show that there is a gap between the generated impressions and reference impressions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: One-shot and few-shot prompts improved conciseness and verisimilitude, but clinicians noted a significant gap between the generated impressions and those written by radiologists.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the gap finding but clarifies it by adding clinician observations and tying it to prompt experiments, so the core result is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Although the LLMs achieve comparable performance in completeness and correctness, the conciseness and verisimilitude scores are not very high.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current LLMs can produce radiology impressions with high completeness and correctness but fall short in conciseness and verisimilitude.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same balance of strengths (completeness/correctness) and weaknesses (conciseness/verisimilitude) with similar certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using few-shot prompts can improve the LLMs' performance in conciseness and verisimilitude.
alignments.pub_claim_text: One-shot and few-shot prompts improved conciseness and verisimilitude, but clinicians noted a significant gap between the generated impressions and those written by radiologists.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the improvement and expands wording to include one-shot prompts and clinician context, so the claim is clarified but consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clinicians believe that LLMs cannot replace radiologists in summarizing the radiology impressions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: One-shot and few-shot prompts improved conciseness and verisimilitude, but clinicians noted a significant gap between the generated impressions and those written by radiologists.
alignments.rationale: The preprint makes a categorical replacement claim; the publication reports clinicians noting a significant gap (a softer formulation), so the strong conclusion about replacement is tempered/expressed more cautiously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural TI allows for thermodynamic integration along an alchemical pathway using a trainable neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural TI allows for thermodynamic integration along an alchemical pathway using a trainable neural network.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The energy-based diffusion model in Neural TI can sample all intermediate ensembles from a single reference calculation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The energy-based diffusion model in Neural TI can sample all intermediate ensembles from a single reference calculation.
alignments.rationale: Identical result presented in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural TI reproduces the underlying changes in free energy without the need for simulations at interpolating Hamiltonians.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural TI reproduces the underlying changes in free energy without the need for simulations at interpolating Hamiltonians.
alignments.rationale: Claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural TI provides accurate calculations of the excess chemical potential for Lennard-Jones fluids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural TI provides accurate calculations of the excess chemical potential for Lennard-Jones fluids.
alignments.rationale: Results are the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We curate a dataset comprising 191 hours of Hindustani Classical Music recordings and annotate it for Raga and tonic labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We curate a dataset comprising 191 hours of Hindustani Classical Music recordings and annotate it for Raga and tonic labels.
alignments.rationale: Dataset contribution is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieve a chunk-wise f1-measure of 0.89 for a subset of 12 Raga classes using a CNN-LSTM model for Automatic Raga Identification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve a chunk-wise f1-measure of 0.89 for a subset of 12 Raga classes using a CNN-LSTM model for Automatic Raga Identification.
alignments.rationale: Performance result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results demonstrate a significant alignment of the model's understanding with human understanding of Ragas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our results demonstrate a significant alignment of the model's understanding with human understanding of Ragas.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion maintained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We employ model explainability techniques SoundLIME and GradCAM++ to evaluate the classifier's predictions against human expert annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We employ model explainability techniques SoundLIME and GradCAM++ to evaluate the alignment of classifier predictions with human expert annotations.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the claim to emphasize evaluation of alignment rather than a generic comparison, a small clarification of wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The incorporation of distribution features of both the current and forthcoming navigation context improves prediction accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of distribution features of both the current and forthcoming navigation context improves prediction accuracy in vessel trajectory prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result, with an explicit domain mention but no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model demonstrates superiority over a previously proposed transformer model for inland vessel trajectory prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model demonstrates superiority over a previously proposed navigation context-sensitive transformer model for inland vessel trajectory prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the comparator more precisely (navigation context-sensitive transformer), but preserves the superiority claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology's generalizability is shown through the usage of data from three distinct river sections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology’s generalizability is demonstrated through the usage of data from three distinct river sections.
alignments.rationale: Generalizability claim is carried over with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach avoids oversimplification of inland navigation patterns by providing the model access to the entire distribution information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach avoids oversimplification of inland navigation patterns by providing the model access to the entire distribution information.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion maintained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Whittle index policy demonstrates guaranteed asymptotic optimality when applied to Restless Multi-Armed Bandit Problems.
alignments.rationale: This specific theoretical claim about guaranteed asymptotic optimality does not appear in the publication's listed claims and thus was removed or at least not carried forward into the public claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QWI, a tabular implementation, converges to the real Whittle indices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QWI, which is a tabular implementation, converges to the real Whittle indices.
alignments.rationale: The tabular convergence claim is restated in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QWINN, an adaptation of QWI using neural networks, is able to extrapolate information from one state to another and scales naturally to large state-space environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QWINN, an adaptation of QWI using neural networks, is able to extrapolate information from one state to another and scales naturally to large state-space environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates this item to an explicit contribution with high certainty (publication labels it a contribution), so the confidence is effectively amplified relative to the preprint's more cautious framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All local minima of the Bellman error for QWINN are locally stable equilibria, which is the first result of its kind for DQN-based schemes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All local minima of the Bellman error for QWINN are locally stable equilibria, which is the first result of its kind for DQN-based schemes.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical stability result is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical computations show that QWI and QWINN converge faster than standard Q-learning and other state of the art algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QWI and QWINN converge faster than the standard Q-learning algorithm, neural-network based approximate Q-learning, and other state-of-the-art algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The numerical convergence speedup claim is present in the publication with equivalent scope and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper introduces a new type of adaptive TinyML solution for on-device learning tasks, specifically for Tiny Speaker Verification (TinySV).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper introduces a new type of adaptive TinyML solution for on-device learning tasks, specifically for Tiny Speaker Verification (TinySV).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution statement verbatim; there is no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed TinySV solution reduces the memory and computational demand of TinyML learning algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed TinySV solution reduces the memory and computational demand of TinyML learning algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication, with no added qualification or change in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TinySV solution is designed to operate with few and possibly unlabelled training data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TinySV solution is designed to operate with few and possibly unlabelled training data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same claim about low-data and possibly unlabelled operation without changing emphasis or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed TinySV solution were evaluated on a dataset collected for the task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness and efficiency of the proposed TinySV solution were evaluated on a dataset collected for the task.
alignments.rationale: Evaluation on a collected dataset is reported in the publication with the same emphasis and no apparent change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution was tested on a real-world IoT device, specifically the Infineon PSoC 62S2 Wi-Fi BT Pioneer Kit.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim list does not include the device-specific testing claim present in the preprint, indicating it was omitted from the final claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel approach for reconstructing historical foehn occurrences using a combination of unsupervised and supervised probabilistic statistical learning methods has been developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel approach for reconstructing historical foehn occurrences using a combination of unsupervised and supervised probabilistic statistical learning methods has been developed.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication, with no change in wording or emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieves accurate hourly reconstructions of north and south foehn occurrence dating back to 1940.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method allows for accurate hourly reconstructions of north and south foehn occurrence dating back to 1940.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the performance/result claim with near-identical wording and intent; no substantive change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This reconstruction method allows for the investigation of how seasonal foehn patterns have evolved over the past 83 years.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This reconstruction method provides valuable insights into climate change impacts on seasonal foehn patterns over the past 83 years.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the investigative capability into an explicit statement about providing insights into climate-change-related pattern shifts, clarifying the applied relevance though not substantially changing temporal scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides valuable insights into climate change impacts on foehn wind events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This reconstruction method provides valuable insights into climate change impacts on seasonal foehn patterns over the past 83 years.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges the climate-impact claim with the seasonal-pattern reconstruction framing, strengthening the explicit linkage to seasonal patterns over the 83-year period and thus revising the original phrasing into a consolidated conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed excitation and aggregation design outperforms the CLIP4Clip results on the MSR-VTT, ActivityNet, and DiDeMo datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed design outperforms the CLIP4Clip results by +1.2% for MSR-VTT, +4.5% for ActivityNet, and +9.5% for DiDeMo.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same outperforming result; the publication gives the exact numeric differences that align with the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The excitation module captures non-mutually-exclusive relationships among frame features and achieves frame-wise features recalibration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed excitation-and-aggregation design improves video representation generation by capturing non-mutually-exclusive relationships among frame features.
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the same functional role of the excitation component (capturing non-mutually-exclusive relationships); wording is slightly broader but consistent with the preprint description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The aggregation module learns exclusiveness used for frame representations aggregation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work addresses the limitations of the mean pooling operation in frame features aggregation.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the aggregation module's role as addressing mean-pooling limitations; this clarifies the motivation and effect of the aggregation behavior described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed modules achieve relative improvements of +1.2%, +4.5%, and +9.5% on the MSR-VTT, ActivityNet, and DiDeMo datasets, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed design outperforms the CLIP4Clip results by +1.2% for MSR-VTT, +4.5% for ActivityNet, and +9.5% for DiDeMo.
alignments.rationale: The numeric relative improvements are repeated directly in the publication, matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DiffUHaul is a training-free method that effectively utilizes the spatial understanding of a localized text-to-image model for the object dragging task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiffUHaul is a training-free method that effectively utilizes the spatial understanding of a localized text-to-image model for the object dragging task.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried over verbatim with no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed attention masking in each denoising step improves editing performance by making the generation more disentangled across different objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed attention masking in each denoising step improves the disentanglement of object representation, leading to better editing performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same result with very similar wording and intent; the claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new diffusion anchoring technique allows for a smooth fusion of new layouts with the original appearance while retaining fine-grained object details.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new diffusion anchoring technique allows for a smooth fusion of new layouts with the original appearance while retaining fine-grained object details.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this technical result identically, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DiffUHaul is adapted to real-image editing through a DDPM self-attention bucketing that enhances the reconstruction of real images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DiffUHaul is adapted to real-image editing through a DDPM self-attention bucketing method that enhances the reconstruction of real images.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the adaptation-to-real-images claim with only minor wording adjustments, leaving the substantive claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy of DiffUHaul is supported by results from an automated evaluation pipeline and a user preference study.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy of DiffUHaul is supported by results from an automated evaluation pipeline and a user preference study.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation evidence claim is maintained exactly in the publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed iterative feature boosting approach enhances machine learning model performance for speech emotion recognition by emphasizing feature relevance and explainability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed iterative feature boosting approach enhances machine learning model performance for speech emotion recognition by emphasizing feature relevance and explainability.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, preserving meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach identifies and removes irrelevant and redundant features, leading to a more effective speech emotion recognition model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach identifies and removes irrelevant and redundant features, leading to a more effective speech emotion recognition model.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated on multiple speech emotion recognition benchmarks, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated on several speech emotion recognition benchmarks, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the benchmarking and outperforming claim with only a minor word change ('multiple' to 'several') that does not alter substantive meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first work to incorporate model explainability into a speech emotion recognition framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first work to incorporate model explainability into a speech emotion recognition framework.
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is preserved in the publication without qualification or retraction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed filtering pipeline refines incomplete 3D human poses from an HPE backbone and a single RGB-D camera.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed filtering pipeline refines incomplete 3D human poses to address challenges in human-robot interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution about refining incomplete 3D human poses; wording is slightly reframed toward HRI but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the proposed filter leads to more consistent and noise-free motion representation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the proposed filter leads to more consistent and noise-free motion representation.
alignments.rationale: The result statement is verbatim between preprint and publication, retaining meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The filtering pipeline reduces unexpected robot movements and enables smoother interaction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The filtering pipeline reduces unexpected robot movements and enables smoother interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this practical result exactly; no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLaVA-generated prompts enhance image-to-image generation by promoting image similarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLaVA-generated prompts promote image similarity in the generation process.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result that LLaVA prompts promote image similarity; core claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant improvement in visual coherence between generated and input images when using LLaVA-generated prompts compared to traditional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significant improvement in visual coherence between the generated and input images compared to traditional methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same comparative improvement in visual coherence; wording and confidence are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future work will explore fine-tuning LLaVA prompts to increase control over the creative process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Future work will explore fine-tuning LLaVA prompts for increased control over the creative process.
alignments.rationale: The planned future direction is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework enhances image-to-image generation by leveraging the multimodal capabilities of the Large Language and Vision Assistant (LLaVA).
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit framing of the overall framework leveraging LLaVA's multimodal capabilities that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a novel feature-centric explanation and evaluation framework for multivariate time series classification named CAFO.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study introduces a novel feature-centric explanation and evaluation framework for multivariate time series classification named CAFO.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about CAFO is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAFO employs a convolution-based approach with channel attention mechanisms, which enhances the separability of attention distributions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAFO employs a convolution-based approach with channel attention mechanisms, which enhances the separability of attention distributions.
alignments.rationale: The methodological/result claim is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The orthogonalization promoted by CAFO refines and stabilizes the ranking of feature importance in multivariate time series classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The orthogonalization promoted by CAFO refines and stabilizes the ranking of feature importance in multivariate time series classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this specific result about orthogonalization's effects on feature ranking.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework's efficacy is validated through extensive empirical analyses on major public benchmarks and real-world datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework's efficacy is validated through extensive empirical analyses on major public benchmarks and real-world datasets.
alignments.rationale: Empirical validation on benchmarks and real-world datasets is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This study advances the understanding of feature-centric explanations in multivariate time series classification and sets a foundation for future explorations.
alignments.rationale: The concluding statement about advancing understanding and future foundation appears in the preprint but is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs have strong yet inconsistent priors in emotion recognition that ossify their predictions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs have strong yet inconsistent priors in emotion recognition that ossify their predictions.
alignments.rationale: The core finding about inconsistent priors and ossified predictions is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The larger the model, the stronger the effects of inconsistent priors become.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The larger the model, the stronger the effects of inconsistent priors on predictions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same result that model size amplifies the inconsistent-prior effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Caution is needed when using ICL with larger LLMs for affect-centered tasks outside their pre-training domain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Caution is needed when using ICL with larger LLMs for affect-centered tasks outside their pretraining domain.
alignments.rationale: The cautionary conclusion about ICL and larger models is present in both versions with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs are unable to fully integrate information from demonstrations that contrast task priors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs are unable to fully integrate information from demonstrations that contrast task priors, leading to performance saturation at suboptimal levels.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit consequence (performance saturation at suboptimal levels) and reduces certainty, clarifying and broadening the original result while tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs significantly reduce the cognitive load associated with navigating vast information resources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The advent of ChatGPT and similar large language models has revolutionized the human-AI interaction and information-seeking process.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific cognitive-load claim into a broader, stronger statement about revolutionizing interaction and information-seeking, expanding scope and amplifying the asserted impact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GOLF framework enhances LLMs' ability to assist in significant life decisions through goal orientation and long-term planning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GOLF framework enhances LLMs' ability to assist in significant life decisions through goal orientation and long-term planning.
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing the GOLF framework is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research demonstrates the transformative potential of LLMs in enhancing human decision-making processes and task management.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By shifting the focus from short-term tasks to the broader spectrum of long-term life goals, this research underscores the transformative potential of LLMs in enhancing human decision-making processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly frames the same transformative potential in the context of long-term goals but preserves the original claim's meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methodology includes a comprehensive simulation study to test the framework's efficacy and develop a dataset benchmark for long-term life tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study includes a comprehensive simulation study to test the framework's efficacy and develop a dataset benchmark for long-term life tasks.
alignments.rationale: The methodological description of a comprehensive simulation study and dataset development is preserved exactly.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This research extends the scope of LLM capabilities beyond short-term task automation to support users in navigating long-term and significant life tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains an explicit statement extending LLM scope to long-term tasks that does not appear as a standalone claim in the preprint.
